questions.question: Why does a byte-level BPE tokenizer start from a 256-symbol alphabet?
options: It guarantees any UTF-8 input can be represented before any merge is learned
It is the smallest alphabet that supports the English language
It matches the GPU warp size on most accelerators
It removes the need to store a merge table
questions.explanation: The 256 raw byte ids cover every possible UTF-8 input. No unknown token is ever required because any input can be expressed as a sequence of bytes.
questions.question_zh: 为什么字节级 BPE tokenizer 从 256 个符号的 alphabet 起步?
options_zh: 保证任何 UTF-8 输入在学习任何 merge 之前就能被表达
这是支持英语的最小 alphabet
这与多数加速器上的 GPU warp size 相同
免去存 merge 表的需要
questions.explanation_zh: 256 个原始字节 id 覆盖任何可能的 UTF-8 输入。任何输入都能表达为字节序列,因此永远不需要 unknown token。

questions.question: What does each BPE training step do?
options: Splits a random word and assigns it a new id
Counts adjacent symbol pairs across the corpus and merges the most frequent one
Decreases the vocabulary by removing the least common id
Trains a small neural network to score subwords
questions.explanation: Each step finds the highest-frequency adjacent pair across the corpus, merges it into a new symbol, and records the merge.
questions.question_zh: BPE 的每一个训练步做什么?
options_zh: 随机切一个词并赋予新 id
在语料上统计相邻符号对,把出现频率最高的一对合并
通过移除最不常见的 id 来缩小词表
训练一个小神经网络给 subword 打分
questions.explanation_zh: 每一步找到语料中频率最高的相邻对,把它合并成一个新符号,并记录这次 merge。

questions.question: When encoding new text, in what order are merges applied?
options: In random order until none apply
By position in the input, left to right, regardless of training rank
By the rank the merge received during training, lowest rank first
By the bytes of the pair, sorted alphabetically
questions.explanation: Inference applies merges in the order they were learned. The earliest merge in the table wins when multiple merges could apply.
questions.question_zh: 在编码新文本时,merge 按什么顺序应用?
options_zh: 随机顺序,直到没有可应用的 merge
按输入位置从左到右,与训练时的 rank 无关
按训练时获得的 rank,rank 最低的优先
按 pair 的字节,字典序排序
questions.explanation_zh: 推理时按 merge 学习时的顺序应用。当多个 merge 都能应用时,merge 表中最早出现的胜出。

questions.question: What is the encoder's behavior on a special-token string when allow_special is False?
options: It raises an error
It maps the string to its reserved id
It encodes the literal bytes of the string
It silently skips the string
questions.explanation: With allow_special off, a string like <|endoftext|> is treated as raw bytes and goes through the merge loop like any other input.
questions.question_zh: 当 allow_special 为 False 时,encoder 对一个 special-token 字符串的行为是?
options_zh: 抛出错误
把它映射到保留的 id
对它的字面字节做编码
静默跳过这个字符串
questions.explanation_zh: 关闭 allow_special 时,像 <|endoftext|> 这样的字符串被当作原始字节,与其他输入一样进入 merge 循环。

questions.question: Why does the decoder never need an unknown-token id?
options: Every learned token is the concatenation of two known tokens, recursing down to single bytes
The encoder strips unknown tokens before decoding
The model rejects unknown ids during sampling
The decoder always returns the empty string for unknown ids
questions.explanation: Every id resolves either to a raw byte or to two previously known ids. Recursive expansion always terminates in bytes, which decode to UTF-8.
questions.question_zh: 为什么 decoder 永远不需要 unknown-token id?
options_zh: 每一个学到的 token 都是两个已知 token 的拼接,递归下降到单字节
encoder 在 decode 之前先剥离 unknown token
模型在采样时拒绝 unknown id
decoder 对 unknown id 总是返回空字符串
questions.explanation_zh: 每个 id 要么解析成一个原始字节,要么解析成两个先前已知的 id。递归展开总会终止于字节,字节再解码为 UTF-8。

questions.question: What happens to the encoded length of a fixed sentence as the target vocabulary grows?
options: It increases linearly with the vocabulary size
It stays constant because each id encodes a single byte
It tends to decrease because larger vocabularies cover more subwords
It oscillates randomly because BPE is non-deterministic
questions.explanation: Larger vocabularies learn more merges, so common subwords collapse into single ids and the encoded sequence gets shorter.
questions.question_zh: 随着目标词表增大,固定句子的 encoded 长度会怎么变化?
options_zh: 随词表大小线性增长
保持不变,因为每个 id 都编码单个字节
倾向于变短,因为更大的词表覆盖更多 subword
随机震荡,因为 BPE 是非确定性的
questions.explanation_zh: 更大的词表学到更多 merge,常见 subword 被合并为单个 id,编码序列因此变短。
